blocks.content: Sard approached the control room entrance, fuming.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Damn it! Just wait till I catch whoever did this!
Only a madman would deactivate the mechanism, so it had to be the work of troublemakers in the school.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Who the hell—!
He grabbed the doorknob and twisted it roughly, but it was locked from the inside.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: You little—!
Expanding his Spirit Zone, he sensed someone leaning against the door inside.
Flames flickered in his palm as he spoke.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Open up. I'll use Fire Strike. If you don't, you'll get blown away too.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: I don't care. No—please, do it, Teacher.
Unexpectedly, it was Maria's voice.
Though not her homeroom teacher, Sard had been at the school before her enrollment, so he couldn't be mistaken.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Maria? What are you doing?

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: I'm sorry, Teacher. Please don't forgive me.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Open the door. Let's talk face to face first.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: I'll be expelled, right? No—maybe I'll go to prison?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: No, Maria. I don't know what's going on, but I'll listen. Just open the door.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: It doesn't matter. I don't want to live anymore. I just want to die here.
Sard extinguished the flames.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Sigh. Fine, stay there if you want.
Given Maria's usual behavior, she wasn't in her right mind. Breaking the door would be pointless.
Leaning against the iron door, Sard gazed at the sky of Alpheus Magic School, which he had once found suffocating in his youth.
His restraint—not yelling at her to come out immediately—calmed Maria's heart slightly.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Teacher.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Do you hate me?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: I don't hate women.
While he generally liked women, this statement reflected his own eccentric philosophy, distinct from that.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Why is the world so unfair?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Haha! You hate unfairness? It's rare to be born as pretty as you.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: You wouldn't understand, Teacher, being a genius mage. What it's like to have no talent. No matter how hard I try, despair always comes first. Do you know how hopeless that feels?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: …I do.
Maria turned her head toward the door.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: You do?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: I graduated at 22, so it's been four years since I became a teacher. You enrolled when I was a senior. You know I'm an alumnus, right?

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Then think back. Have you ever heard of a student named Sard at this school?
Now that he mentioned it, she couldn't recall anything.
Even Shirone, who became the talk of the school immediately after enrolling, was an exception.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: No. Never.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Figures. Middle-to-lower grades, no talent, not from a wealthy family—just a troublemaker always fighting outside school.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: You?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Yep. I took two years for every class jump. Spent three years kneeling in the senior class. Haha! Though my situation was slightly better than yours now.
Sard imagined Maria's pout as he spoke.
But isn't it the same? Unless you're the best, everyone breaks eventually. I was no different. I made it to the senior class but failed every time.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Then how…?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: How did I become a top teacher? Luck, pure luck. I did my best, but not as much as others, and I hated struggling to crush others. Then one day, someone told me this.
Sard recalled the moment.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: They said, 'The reason you can't win competitions isn't because you can't—it's because you don't want to.'

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Maria quietly repeated the words.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Maria, you don't have to beat anyone. If you love magic, that's enough. There are people in this world who don't want to compete. Like me.
The ten-year-old girl who had enrolled in school because she loved magic was now broken after nine years.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Meeting that person changed my life. Thinking back, they were such a weirdo. A teacher saying something like that.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Was that person… you?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Yes. My mentor, Headmaster Alpheus.
Maria's head whipped around.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: What? The Headmaster?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: From that day on, I became their formal student and received personal guidance. An incredible privilege. It turned my life around. With training from a mage renowned across the continent, even a scoundrel like me could be called a genius and live comfortably. How about it? Envious? Life's all luck, hahaha!
Maria grew despondent again.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: So what I'm saying is, the world is unfair. Some are born talented, some throw themselves into competition. But even someone like me—untalented and hating competition—can sometimes experience miracles. That's why everyone lives with hope, right?
Sard stood up and turned away.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Miracles can come to anyone. It's too soon to give up after all the time you've spent. Why not try a little longer? Just a little?
Maria buried her face in her knees.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Should I stake my life on a miracle that might never come? What if it doesn't? What happens to me then?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: What are you talking about, Maria?
Sard placed a hand on the iron door and smiled.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Look—a miracle has already come to you.
Tears welled in Maria's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Let's start over. You might not have soared like others, but don't define yourself yet. Maybe you were just unlucky. Maybe the method was wrong. School isn't efficient for everyone. So, can you trust me one more time? I'll definitely make you a mage.
Maria covered her face with her hands.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Teacher, really? Can I really do it?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: You can. If magic is your life, never give up.
With a clank, the lock opened. Maria emerged, her eyes swollen, and shouted urgently.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Teacher! Hurry! The mechanism… the mechanism…!

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Huh? The mechanism?
Even leaping 700 meters, teleportation magic wouldn't take long. By now, the test should be nearing its end, and the lack of news suggested the teachers had handled it smoothly.
At that moment, defying Sard's expectations, a sharp sound pierced the air as a streak of light crashed down.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Teacher Sard! What are you doing!
Siena charged forward, her eyes blazing with a mix of fury and urgency.
As she grabbed Sard's collar, she noticed Maria and froze in confusion.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Maria? Why are you here?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: What's wrong? Did something happen?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Deactivate the mechanism now! The uncrossable bridge's difficulty is set to Level 10!

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: What?!
It wasn't just the safety measures that had been tampered with?
Remembering Maria's words, Sard realized the truth and rushed toward the control room.
Just then, another streak of light arced down.
Etella arrived via spatial teleportation, panting as she ran toward Siena and Sard.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Teacher! It's terrible, just terrible!
Given the scale of the trap, Siena was terrified.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: What's wrong? Don't tell me there's been an accident…?

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Shirone… Shirone is…!
